project.name: Avelo
project.description: Offline macOS accounting application for Indian businesses

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore rules for the Avelo project. Excludes macOS system files (.DS_Store, .Trashes), Xcode/Swift build artifacts (*.xcodeproj, DerivedData, .build, .swiftpm), editor configs (.vscode, .idea), and the opencode project config directory.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Master agent instructions for taking Avelo to a release-grade macOS application. Defines the authority and source-of-truth document hierarchy (PRD, Rules, Naming Freeze, Architecture, Status, Execution, Release Board, DX). Covers project structure and dependency boundaries (App, Features, Core/Services, Core/Repositories, Core/Database, Core/Models, Core/Validation, Core/Utilities, Shared, Resources, Tests, Vendor/SQLCipher). Specifies non-negotiable implementation rules: fully offline, SQLite as source of truth, Int64 paise money model, double-entry accounting, typed AppError, @MainActor @Observable view models. Lays out database/migration/backup/restore contracts, Swift and UI conventions, supported make targets, verification gates by change type, definition of done, documentation synchronization requirements, working-tree discipline, and the release-grade gate criteria.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Tracks unreleased changes for Avelo v1.1 (build 3). Documents the Tally item-invoice mode for Sales/Purchase with GST auto-calculation and structured item lines. Records item-invoice posting atomicity fix via ItemInvoiceService transaction wrapping. Notes MigrationV021 for voucher draft account_ledger_id persistence. Lists completed P0 release items (AVL-P0-014 through AVL-P0-023). Covers migration notes for make-based workflow and Scripts/swiftw.sh caching. Documents SQLCipher-backed encrypted company databases, hardened backup/restore with manifest validation, preserved 500-draft chunk semantics, encryption-aware performance benchmarks, and first-run bootstrap improvements.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Contributor guidelines for the Avelo project. Defines branch naming conventions (feature/, fix/, docs/, chore/ prefixed), commit style (short imperative subjects), local developer loop (make setup, make test, make verify), test expectations (behavior-driven names, regression tests for bugs, Tests/AveloTests/ directory), and pull request requirements (summary, commands run, screenshots for UI changes, rule/migration impact callouts, README/DX/CHANGELOG updates for release-path changes).

nodes.name: LICENSE
nodes.summary: Proprietary software license. Copyright 2026 Karbonteck. All rights reserved. Unauthorized copying, modification, distribution, or use is strictly prohibited without prior written permission.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Primary build automation for the Avelo project. Contains merge conflicts between HEAD and origin/main branches. Targets include: setup (bootstrap.sh), dev (debug build and launch), build (swift build), bundle (release binary + dist/Avelo.app assembly), validate-bundle, launch-smoke, bundle-selftest, benchmark, benchmark-million, verify (rule-audit + test + bundle + validate + selftest), test (full test suite), net-check (zero network calls gate), r16-check (ObservableObject/@Published forbidden), r15-check (no TODOs/fatalError), r4-check (no Double/Float in money paths), rule-audit (combines all rule checks), board/todo/count (task board queries), and help. Uses Scripts/swiftw.sh wrapper for SwiftPM cache isolation.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README for Avelo, an offline-first double-entry accounting application for macOS. Contains merge conflicts between HEAD and origin/main. Describes the app as native Swift 5.9 + SwiftUI with raw SQLite3, no third-party packages, no network calls. Documents features: multi-company storage, Int64 paise double-entry accounting, 8 voucher types, 10+ report types, inventory with batch tracking, payroll, banking reconciliation, SQLCipher encryption, backup/restore with SHA-256 manifests, audit logging, and financial year locking. Includes performance benchmarks (10k voucher post in 5.226s, 100k in 97.805s, 50k reports under 1s). Provides quick start, build instructions, data location (~/Library/Application Support/Avelo/), and architecture layer map.

nodes.name: Avelo Assembly
nodes.summary: Complete file inventory of the Avelo project. Lists every file by directory with its role, data flow overview, and maps the layered architecture from SwiftUI views through ViewModels, services, repositories, and SQLite. References all normative documents and serves as the entry point for navigating the codebase.

nodes.name: Avelo Accounting Logic Audit
nodes.summary: Read-only accounting correctness review of the current worktree. Documents an account-semantics matrix, workflow Dr/Cr expectations, and seven audit findings (P&L zero-amount rows, balance-sheet group omission, GST taxable-base mislabeling, report filter inconsistencies, cash-flow detection bypass, stock-ageing company scoping, UI silent arithmetic failure conversion). Records confirmed strengths and regression scenario catalogue.

nodes.name: Avelo Architecture
nodes.summary: Normative architecture document defining the layered model (App, Features, Core/Services, Core/Repositories, Core/Database, Core/Models, Shared, Resources), dependency direction, composition root, threading model, database access pattern (SQLiteDatabase wrapper, DatabaseManager actor, CompanyHandle), MVVM pattern, error handling contract (AppError), money formatting (Currency enum), routing, multi-company plumbing, the accounting write pipeline, report query patterns, backup/restore contracts, keyboard state machine, and the in-memory AccountTree cache.

nodes.name: Avelo Execution Checklist
nodes.summary: Remaining-work execution queue tracking only unfinished AVL-* backlog items from the Release Board. Groups items into dependency-ordered waves (P0-A foundational proof closure, P0-B accounting/fiscal blockers, P0-C storage/UI/legal, P1-A accountant workflow, P1-B compliance, P1-C reliability/printing/import, P2 post-launch polish). Includes release gate policy, evidence templates, and detailed proof logs for advanced P0 items.

nodes.name: Avelo Master PRD
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for product behavior. Defines the product (offline native macOS accounting for Indian small businesses), non-negotiables, user personas, glossary, data model summary, default chart of accounts (13 groups, 28 ledgers, 10 voucher types), voucher type rules, and exhaustive screen-by-screen specifications for CompanyPickerView, CompanySetupView, OpeningBalancesView, DashboardView, AccountsHomeView, VouchersHomeView, VoucherEntryView, ReportsHomeView, all 8 report types, InventoryHomeView, PayrollHomeView, BankReconciliationView, AuditLogView, SettingsView, cross-cutting UX (keyboard shortcuts, date/money formats, empty states, banners, confirmations), build phases, and explicitly excluded future work.

nodes.name: Avelo Master Product Execution Plan
nodes.summary: Single authoritative product roadmap and dependency-ordered execution plan. Defines product direction (native macOS shell + Tally-style accounting + checked encrypted engine + no-code extensions), locked decisions, architecture contracts, central account eligibility policy, declarative definitions, contextual action registry, product UX vision (shell/gateway, masters, voucher workbench, inventory/manufacturing/orders/banking, reports/charts/dashboards, documents/exports), keyboard compatibility matrix, reliability/security/schema requirements, and phased execution from Phase 0 (v1.1 correctness) through Phase 10 (Extension Language).

nodes.name: Avelo Module Checklist
nodes.summary: File-by-file gap map and per-slice acceptance criteria. Lists every Swift file in the project organized by pass (Docs, Shared/Theme, Models, Database, Resources, Repositories, Services, Validation, App shell, Features, Project root) with done/pending/deferred status. Includes per-slice acceptance criteria for Foundation, Accounts, Vouchers, Reports, Inventory, Banking/backup/audit, Payroll, and Hardening slices.

nodes.name: Avelo Naming Freeze
nodes.summary: Locked type, method, table, and column names for the entire codebase. Covers App/environment types, Core models (Company, FinancialYear, AccountGroup, Account, VoucherType, Voucher, LedgerLine, InventoryItem, StockMovement, PayrollEmployee, AuditEvent, ReportResult DTOs), Database layer types, Repository methods, Service responsibilities, Validation types, Utilities, UI naming conventions, full enum case lists (AuditAction, ValidationErrorCode, InventoryLinkMode, EntrySide, MovementType, ValuationMethod, VoucherType.Code), SQL table and column definitions, and keyboard shortcut identifiers.

nodes.name: Avelo Operator Guide
nodes.summary: Operator-facing guide covering backup/restore procedures, encryption at rest, reports availability, inventory features, search behavior, and explicitly excluded features (group consolidation, GST portal upload, cloud sync, UUIDv7 migration). Contains merge conflicts between HEAD and origin/main branches.

nodes.name: Avelo Phase 0 Manual Acceptance
nodes.summary: Canonical human acceptance record required before Phase 0 closes. Defines artifact-bound acceptance protocol with sections: A (Accountant A1-A5: company/FY/account selection, voucher keyboard/posting, bills/cheques/corrections/audit, reports/tax/dates, inventory baseline), B (Operator B1-B2: backup/restore/keys/corruption, migration/sleep/storage/resources), C (Accessibility/visual/PDF/print), D (Distribution/clean-machine). States are PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED/NOT RUN. Distribution section is BLOCKED due to missing Developer ID identity.

nodes.name: Avelo Release Board
nodes.summary: Canonical P0/P1/P2 readiness catalogue and release verdict (currently NOT READY FOR PUBLIC PRODUCTION). Defines severity rules, state rules, release split (V1 must-ship, V2 should-ship, V3 hardening), and lists 37 P0 items, 44 P1 items, and 20 P2 items with status, dependencies, requirements, and proof-of-done criteria. Includes shortcut compatibility matrix and historical RC board entries.

nodes.name: Avelo Rules
nodes.summary: Non-negotiable rules that every code-generation pass, review, and change must hold. Covers 18 rules: R-1 100% offline, R-2 manual entry only, R-3 SQLite as system of record, R-4 Int64 paise (never Double), R-5 double-entry mandatory, R-6 FY boundaries/locks, R-7 inventory optional, R-8 auto-link not silent, R-9 audit logging, R-10 no silent deletion, R-11 reversal-only for locked FY, R-12 multi-company isolated files, R-13 reports as pure functions, R-14 naming freeze, R-15 no placeholders/TODOs, R-16 SwiftUI lifecycle/@Observable, R-17 typed errors, R-18 keyboard-first.

nodes.name: Avelo Schema
nodes.summary: Frozen SQLite schema defining all 17 tables (avelo_companies, avelo_financial_years, avelo_account_groups, avelo_accounts, avelo_voucher_types, avelo_vouchers, avelo_ledger_lines, avelo_inventory_items, avelo_stock_movements, avelo_payroll_employees, avelo_payroll_entries, avelo_audit_events, avelo_voucher_sequences, avelo_voucher_templates, avelo_bank_reconciliations, avelo_migrations, and registry tables) with columns, types, constraints, indexes, triggers (FY overlap rejection, group-must-be-leaf, voucher date/FY lock enforcement, ledger line lock inheritance, audit immutability), pragmas, and migration policy.

nodes.name: Avelo Status Checklist
nodes.summary: Human-readable progress checklist complementing the Release Board and Execution Checklist. Records current worktree evidence (voucher posting stabilization, audit-chain safeguards, benchmark results, 498-test pass, rule audit, RC bundle, launch smoke), foundation/safety status, core bookkeeping/reporting status, inventory/banking/operational status, v1.1 release gate next steps (automated proof, accountant/operator/keyboard/accessibility/visual acceptance, distribution path), and future phase plans.

nodes.name: DX Developer Experience
nodes.summary: Developer experience and release evidence documentation. Defines documentation ownership matrix (which document owns what), supported commands (make setup/build/test/dev, make rule-audit/rc-local/verify/benchmark/launch-smoke), repo-local SwiftPM state management, rule and release-candidate proof workflow, benchmark guidelines, evidence record format, and public-production distribution runbook (Developer ID and Mac App Store paths).

nodes.name: Tally Master Study Guide
nodes.summary: Exhaustive Tally feature checklist and Avelo gap analysis. Maps every recoverable Tally menu, screen, and workflow surface (Gateway, Company Info, Accounts Info, Inventory Info, Payroll Info, Voucher types, Reports, Settings/F11/F12) with status labels (implemented/partial/missing/deferred). Includes workflow checklists by business task, shortcut checklist, Windows-to-macOS UX mapping, Avelo gap analysis (implemented, partially implemented, missing, out of scope), codebase cross-check, and beginner learning path.

nodes.name: SQLiteDatabase
nodes.summary: Low-level SQLite wrapper using raw sqlite3 C API. Provides prepared statement caching, transaction management with nested BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK, row-level query helpers, WAL pragmas, checkpointing, and vacuum. All database access flows through this class.

nodes.name: DatabaseManager
nodes.summary: Actor-based manager that owns the application support directory, registry database, and per-company SQLite handles. Manages company file creation, opening, closing, migration on open, and registry CRUD operations.

nodes.name: SchemaVersion
nodes.summary: Enum defining the database schema version (currently v1 and v2). Provides a comparable current version used by MigrationRunner and backup/restore validation.

nodes.name: MigrationRunner
nodes.summary: Applies forward-only schema migrations to a SQLiteDatabase. Tracks applied versions via avelo_migrations table, runs unapplied migrations in version order, and updates PRAGMA user_version.

nodes.name: SeedLoader
nodes.summary: Loads default chart of accounts (Tally-compatible 28 groups, 34 ledgers, voucher types) into a newly created company database. Validates group hierarchy for cycles and nature consistency before inserting.

nodes.name: BackupService
nodes.summary: Exports a company database to a backup file with SHA-256 checksum manifest. Checkpoints WAL before copy, writes atomic temp files, and records audit events for backup export.

nodes.name: RestoreService
nodes.summary: Restores a company from backup. Validates manifest and checksum, stages the file, opens with optional recovery key, runs migrations, remaps company IDs, rebuilds audit chain integrity, recreates triggers, and registers the company. Handles legacy plaintext and passphrase-encrypted databases.

nodes.name: RecoveryKeyCodec
nodes.summary: Encodes and decodes recovery keys using Base32 with AV1 versioned envelope format. Includes SHA-256 checksum validation and legacy key normalization.

nodes.name: CompanyKeyStore
nodes.summary: Stores per-company encryption keys in the macOS Keychain (SecItem API). Provides generate/store/retrieve/delete operations with in-memory test double.

nodes.name: LegacyKeyMigrationService
nodes.summary: Migrates legacy unencrypted or hardcoded-passphrase databases to per-company key encryption using sqlcipher_export ATTACH/DETACH. Verifies table list and row counts match after migration.

nodes.name: AccountGroupRepository
nodes.summary: CRUD for avelo_account_groups. Supports hierarchical queries (roots, children, leaf groups), company-scoped listing, and row-to-model mapping.

nodes.name: AccountRepository
nodes.summary: CRUD for avelo_accounts. Supports lookup by ID/code/codes, company-scoped listing (all, active, bank accounts, by group), insert, update, disable, and last-used tracking.

nodes.name: AccountingWorkflowsRepository
nodes.summary: Manages bill allocations (avelo_bill_allocations) and cheques (avelo_cheques). Provides workflow input assembly, cheque register with party/voucher joins, and bill allocation queries with date/party filters.

nodes.name: AuditRepository
nodes.summary: Appends and queries avelo_audit_events. Supports filtered listing with action, entity type/id, text search, date range, pagination, and entity count.

nodes.name: BOMRepository
nodes.summary: Manages bills of materials (avelo_boms) and components (avelo_bom_components). Supports upsert, component replacement, list with assembly item join, component edge listing, and editability validation.

nodes.name: BankReconciliationRepository
nodes.summary: Manages bank reconciliations (avelo_bank_reconciliations) and statement lines (avelo_bank_statement_lines). Provides upsert, statement line CRUD, voucher candidate queries, book balance calculation, and clearing operations.

nodes.name: CompanyRepository
nodes.summary: CRUD for avelo_companies. Supports find by ID, list for registry, insert, update, and disable. Used by DatabaseManager and RestoreService.

nodes.name: FinancialYearOpeningBalanceRepository
nodes.summary: Manages avelo_financial_year_opening_balances for year-close carried-forward balances. Supports list, replace (delete+insert), and signed balance calculation.

nodes.name: FinancialYearRepository
nodes.summary: CRUD for avelo_financial_years. Supports find by ID, list for company, find open years, find most recent, insert, lock/unlock, and mark closed.

nodes.name: InventoryOrderRepository
nodes.summary: Manages inventory orders (avelo_inventory_orders) and lines (avelo_inventory_order_lines). Supports insert with lines in transaction, list/find orders, line queries, fulfillment updates, status updates, pending lines, reorder level management, and reorder alerts.

nodes.name: InventoryRepository
nodes.summary: CRUD for inventory items (avelo_inventory_items) and stock movements (avelo_stock_movements). Supports item filtering with search/pagination, movement insertion/queries, running balance calculation via InventoryValuationEngine replay, and movement chronologically sorted listing.

nodes.name: LedgerLineRepository
nodes.summary: CRUD for avelo_ledger_lines. Provides batch insert (chunked at 99 rows per statement), voucher-scoped queries, deletion, and debit/credit aggregation with date and reversal filters.

nodes.name: MasterDataRepository
nodes.summary: Manages cost centres (avelo_cost_centres), cost categories (avelo_cost_categories), and budgets (avelo_budgets). CRUD operations for deferred features (cost centres are placeholder-only in HEAD branch).

nodes.name: PartyProfileRepository
nodes.summary: CRUD for avelo_party_profiles. Supports find by account+company, list for company, usage lookup map, and upsert with credit limit/period validation.

nodes.name: PayrollRepository
nodes.summary: Manages payroll employees (avelo_payroll_employees) and entries (avelo_payroll_entries). Supports employee CRUD with deactivation/termination, entry insertion, and filtered entry listing with employee name joins.

layers.name: Application Layer
layers.description: App entry point, lifecycle, composition root, and routing. The outermost shell that wires dependencies and launches the UI.

layers.name: Feature Layer
layers.description: SwiftUI views and @MainActor @Observable view models organized by domain feature (Accounts, Vouchers, Reports, Inventory, Banking, Payroll, Dashboard, Settings, Onboarding, Audit, GST).

layers.name: Shared UI Layer
layers.description: Reusable SwiftUI components, theme tokens, and formatting helpers consumed across features.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Business logic, validation orchestration, transactional write pipelines, and accounting rules. Services own business decisions and coordinate repositories.

layers.name: Validation Layer
layers.description: Reusable business validators, error types (AppError), and account eligibility policies.

layers.name: Repository Layer
layers.description: Data access layer. SQL queries, row-to-model mapping, and persistence operations. Repositories do not invent business rules.

layers.name: Database Core
layers.description: SQLite access, DatabaseManager, seed loading

layers.name: Domain Models
layers.description: Core domain types: Company, FinancialYear, Account, AccountGroup, Audit

layers.name: Actions Layer
layers.description: App-wide action definitions and the contextual action registry for keyboard shortcuts and command routing.

layers.name: Keyboard Layer
layers.description: Keyboard monitoring, shortcut state machine, and keyboard-to-action bridging.

layers.name: Cache Layer
layers.description: In-memory caching for computed financial structures (AccountTree) to avoid repeated SQLite queries.

layers.name: Utilities Layer
layers.description: Focused cross-cutting utilities: checked arithmetic, currency formatting, date formatters, exact quantity, and backup exclusion helpers.

layers.name: Resources Layer
layers.description: SQL schema definitions, seed data (chart of accounts), and bundled assets consumed at runtime.

layers.name: Vendor Layer
layers.description: Vendored and version-pinned third-party source (SQLCipher). Requires explicit review for security, licence, build, and maintenance.

layers.name: Scripts Layer
layers.description: Build scripts, benchmark harnesses, bootstrap setup, bundle assembly, and SwiftPM cache isolation wrappers.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Normative project documents: PRD, architecture, rules, naming freeze, schema, release board, execution checklist, operator guide, and changelog.

layers.name: CI/CD Configuration
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows, issue templates, and PR templates.

layers.name: Project Configuration
layers.description: Project-level config files not in other categories

layers.name: Financial Models
layers.description: Voucher, ledger, draft, workspace configuration, and reporting types

layers.name: Inventory & BOM Models
layers.description: Inventory items, stock movements, orders, and bill-of-materials types

layers.name: Build Configuration
layers.description: Package.swift, Makefile, linting rules, and build system config

layers.name: Normative Documents
layers.description: PRD, architecture specs, naming freeze, status checklists, operator guides

layers.name: Schema & Migrations
layers.description: SQL schema definitions and forward-only migration scripts (V002-V025)

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Avelo is an offline-first double-entry accounting app for macOS. Native Swift 5.9 + SwiftUI, raw SQLite3, zero third-party packages, zero network calls. All data lives locally under ~/Library/Application Support/Avelo/. Start here to understand the project scope and constraints.

tour.title: The Rules That Never Bend
tour.description: Before writing or reviewing any code, internalize the 18 non-negotiable rules: fully offline, Int64 paise for money (never Double), double-entry mandatory, typed AppError, @Observable (never ObservableObject), keyboard-first, no silent deletion. These rules gate every PR.

tour.title: Architecture at a Glance
tour.description: The layered architecture enforces one-way dependency flow: App → Features → Services → Repositories → Database → Models. Views never touch SQLite directly. Services own business decisions. Repositories do not invent rules. Read this to understand the boundaries.

tour.title: Entry Point — main.swift
tour.description: The app starts here. main.swift dispatches to SelfTestHarness when --self-test is passed (used by bundle validation scripts), otherwise launches AveloApp.main(). This is the only file in the Application Layer.

tour.title: App Lifecycle — AveloApp.swift
tour.description: AveloApp conforms to SwiftUI App protocol. It creates the WindowGroup, sets up the AppEnvironment (composition root), and launches the root navigation. This is where the dependency graph is constructed.

tour.title: Composition Root — AppEnvironment.swift
tour.description: AppEnvironment is the single place where all services, repositories, and database connections are wired together. It owns the DatabaseManager, creates services with their dependencies, and injects them into the view hierarchy via environment. No other file should construct the dependency graph.

tour.title: Navigation — AppRouter and SidebarView
tour.description: AppRouter manages the active screen state and navigation destinations. SidebarView renders the module picker (Dashboard, Accounts, Vouchers, Reports, Inventory, Banking, Payroll, Audit, Settings). SidebarDestination maps sidebar selections to concrete views. This is how users move through the app.

tour.title: Root View and Company Selection
tour.description: RootView orchestrates the top-level UI: company picker on first launch, then the main sidebar+content split. CompanyPickerView lets users select or create a company. Each company gets its own isolated SQLCipher-encrypted database file.

tour.title: Core Models
tour.description: Plain value types with no service or UI dependencies. Key models: Company, FinancialYear, Account, AccountGroup, Voucher, VoucherDraft, LedgerLine, InventoryItem, PayrollEmployee, AuditEvent, and ReportResult DTOs. Money is always Int64 paise. Enums like EntrySide, VoucherType.Code, and AuditAction are defined here.

tour.title: Database Layer — SQLiteDatabase and DatabaseManager
tour.description: SQLiteDatabase wraps raw sqlite3 C calls with Swift-safe APIs. DatabaseManager (an actor) serializes all database access per company, manages WAL mode, runs the migration runner, and ensures thread safety. CompanyHandle provides the database reference passed through the entire call chain.

tour.title: Schema and Migrations
tour.description: schema_v1.sql defines all 15+ tables with triggers for FY lock enforcement, date validation, and audit immutability. MigrationRunner applies forward-only migrations (V002–V025). Each migration is a standalone Swift file. The schema is frozen — changes require explicit approval.

tour.title: Backup, Restore, and Encryption
tour.description: BackupService creates SHA-256–manifested backups. RestoreService validates manifests, stages, and atomically replaces company databases. CompanyKeyStore manages SQLCipher encryption keys via Keychain. RecoveryKeyCodec encodes/decodes human-readable recovery keys.

tour.title: Repository Layer
tour.description: Repositories own SQL queries and row-to-model mapping. They do not invent business rules. Key repositories: AccountRepository, VoucherRepository, LedgerLineRepository, ReportRepository (with extensions for financial statements, compliance reports, and GST), FinancialYearRepository, InventoryRepository, AuditRepository. Each talks to exactly one database.

tour.title: Validation Layer
tour.description: Reusable validators enforce business constraints before writes. AppError is the single typed error type thrown by services. VoucherDraftValidator checks voucher completeness. AccountInputValidator and CompanyInputValidator validate user input. AccountEligibilityPolicy determines which accounts can receive ledger entries.

tour.title: Service Layer — The Business Brain
tour.description: Services own business decisions and transactional orchestration. VoucherService posts vouchers with double-entry enforcement. TransactionService wraps multi-step writes atomically. FinancialYearService handles FY creation, locking, and closing. AuditService writes immutable audit events. ReportService computes reports as pure functions from persisted data.

tour.title: Accounting Write Pipeline
tour.description: The core accounting flow: validate draft → check FY lock → acquire sequence number → insert voucher + ledger lines atomically → write audit event → update caches. This pipeline is enforced in VoucherService and TransactionService. Every write must pass through it.

tour.title: Feature — Dashboard
tour.description: DashboardView shows the financial overview: cash, bank, receivables, payables, sales, GST payable, stock value. DashboardViewModel fetches summary data from ReportService. This is the first screen users see after company selection.

tour.title: Feature — Voucher Entry
tour.description: VouchersView lists all vouchers with filtering and search. VoucherEntryView (via NewVoucherSheet) is the primary data entry surface — a keyboard-driven form for creating vouchers. VoucherEditViewModel manages draft state. VoucherType defines the 8 supported voucher types (Sales, Purchase, Receipt, Payment, Journal, Contra, Credit Note, Debit Note).

tour.title: Feature — Reports
tour.description: ReportsView provides 10+ report types: Trial Balance, Balance Sheet, P&L, Day Book, Ledger, GST Summary, Outstanding, Stock Valuation, and more. Reports are computed as pure functions from persisted ledger lines — never cached totals. ReportsViewModel coordinates report selection and parameter filtering.

tour.title: Feature — Inventory
tour.description: InventoryView manages items, stock movements, and reorder alerts. InventoryViewModel tracks stock levels. BOMsView handles bill-of-materials for manufacturing. OrdersView manages purchase/sale orders with line items. Inventory is an optional feature gated by CompanyFeatureSet.

tour.title: Shared Components
tour.description: Reusable UI building blocks consumed across all features. AccountPicker for account selection, MoneyTextField for Int64 paise input, SearchBar for filtering, EmptyStateView for empty lists, ErrorBanner for typed error display, ModuleChrome for consistent module headers, and KeyboardShortcutMap for shortcut registration.

tour.title: Keyboard System
tour.description: Avelo is keyboard-first. KeyboardMonitor captures global key events. KeyboardRouter maps shortcuts to AppActions. KeyboardBridge connects the keyboard system to SwiftUI focus states. KeyboardShortcutMap registers shortcuts in the UI. This system enables Tally-style function-key navigation.

tour.title: Utilities and Formatting
tour.description: Focused cross-cutting utilities. CheckedMath provides Int64 arithmetic that fails on overflow instead of wrapping. Currency formats paise to display strings. DateFormatters handles Indian date formats. IndianFinancialYear resolves FY boundaries. FiscalLockChecker prevents writes to locked periods.

tour.title: Build System and Verification
tour.description: The Makefile provides all build targets: make dev (debug), make test (full suite), make verify (release confidence), make rule-audit (automated rule checks), make bundle (release binary). Scripts/swiftw.sh wraps swift for isolated SwiftPM caches. Scripts/bootstrap.sh runs first-time setup.

tour.title: Testing Strategy
tour.description: Tests live in Tests/AveloTests/ and cover unit, service, repository, migration, restore, view-model, and regression scenarios. Use real temporary SQLite databases, not mocks. Behavior-driven test names (e.g., testPostingLockedFYFails). Run make test for the full suite, or Scripts/swiftw.sh test --filter for targeted runs.

tour.title: Normative Documents — The Source of Truth
tour.description: When two documents conflict, the hierarchy is: PRD → Rules → Naming Freeze → Architecture → Status → Execution → Release Board. The Naming Freeze locks all type, method, table, and column names. The Schema document freezes the SQLite structure. The Release Board tracks readiness with P0/P1/P2 severity.

tour.title: Release Readiness
tour.description: Avelo is currently NOT READY FOR PUBLIC PRODUCTION. The release gate requires: no open P0 blockers, full test suite passing, warning-free release build, bundle assembly passing, benchmark thresholds met, and manual accountant/operator/keyboard/accessibility acceptance recorded. Check the Execution Checklist for next concrete actions.
